steps.title: Identify the three points from the coordinate grid
Find the center of the circle
steps.content: Points $(7, 11)$ and $(7, 5)$ are vertically aligned at $x = 7$ and symmetric about $y = 8$. The center must have $y$-coordinate 8. Since $(4, 8)$ is on the circle and on the line $y = 8$, and all three points are equidistant from the center, the center is at $(7, 8)$.

steps.title: Calculate the circumference and find k
steps.content:
Each point is distance 3 from $(7, 8)$:
  • $(7, 8)$ to $(4, 8)$: distance $= 3$
  • $(7, 8)$ to $(7, 11)$: distance $= 3$
  • $(7, 8)$ to $(7, 5)$: distance $= 3$
With radius $r = 3$:
$$C = 2\pi r = 2\pi(3) = 6\pi$$
Therefore $k = 6$. That matches choice B—B is correct.
